data.description:
## Why
Fan-out steps are I/O-bound LLM calls; serial execution wastes latency. Bounded concurrency is feasible in v1
with deterministic replay preserved, because iteration-keyed markers make map iterations order-independent.

## What
Execute map iterations with BOUNDED concurrency (parallel LLM calls), serializing event commits through the
existing store lock. Preserve deterministic replay. Relax the Burr tripwire NARROWLY for the map path only, with
a recorded rationale; the async/threading ban stays in force everywhere else.

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py (map execution)
- tests/unit/workflow/test_executor_tripwire.py (scoped relaxation + rationale)
Out: parallelism outside the map path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Map runs N agent steps concurrently with a configurable bound; event commits serialized.
- [ ] Deterministic replay holds under concurrency (order-independent via iteration markers).
- [ ] Tripwire permits bounded concurrency in the map path ONLY, with recorded rationale; ban intact elsewhere.
data.title: Bounded-concurrent fan-out (parallel map)
